summary: Stancil recounts how, as a data analyst cofounding a startup with no product or data, he found himself without a clear role and started a blog to explore quirky data stories. Years later, he’s relaunching that impulse on Substack, committing to regular posts that mix data culture and tech commentary with his eclectic interests.
money_quotes: On August 27, I realized why data analysts like myself aren’t typically among the first employees at tech startups—you don’t have much to do.
A day in, I was a founder with a company, but without a job.
So, as I think the saying goes, those who can, do; those who can’t, start a blog.
Because Mode’s now well-manicured, professional company blog wasn’t the right place to rant about how social media is dooming society, this Substack was born.
Expect predictions about data technologies and observations about corporate data culture alongside the regularly scheduled commentary on Pitbull.
key_insight: The Substack is a renewed, more regular outlet for Stancil’s mix of data-driven analysis and broader tech-cultural commentary, shaped by his experience building a data-focused company.
